summary: Frontend polish-pass verification for Mr. Maybe Flight. Verified SVG paper plane (origami two-tone wings + dark fold + tail fin) renders cleanly on home and game, SVG puffy clouds render in 3 home positions and parallax layers in game, ring renders with yellow color (pulse runs in code each frame via Math.sin), all HUD testIDs (score-display, tilt-indicator, speed-indicator, pause-button, touch-capture) work, Pause -> Resume / Quit and ready overlay FLY transition all functional, plane idle bob animation visible on home. Fixed 3 remaining `pointerEvents` props in game.tsx that were still firing the deprecation warning (lines 479/494/767). After fix: shadow*/textShadow* warnings = 0; pointerEvents warning down from several to 1 (likely from third-party lib SafeAreaView/expo-router internals).

ui_bugs.issue: Score popup +50 is COLLECTED into popupsRef on ring pickup but is NEVER RENDERED in the JSX tree. The styles.scorePopup style is defined but no <Text> uses popupsRef.current. Result: the +50 floating popup the polish pass claims to add never appears on screen.

Crash flash overlay code is correct (280ms pink overlay on collision). Could not be exercised on the web preview because sensors don't fire on web => plane stays centered and rarely intersects an obstacle within a short test window. Suggest exposing a debug trigger or a deterministic obstacle path for QA, OR test on a real device.

react_native_issues.issue: 1 residual `props.pointerEvents is deprecated` console warning remains after the fix. Our 3 in-source occurrences were migrated to style.pointerEvents. The remaining single firing appears to come from a third-party (likely SafeAreaView/expo-router). Acceptable but worth noting.

action_items: Render popupsRef in JSX: map popupsRef.current to <Text> nodes positioned at the plane's screen coords with a translateY animation based on (now - p.t)/1000 so the +50 actually appears and floats up. Currently the polish-pass score popup feature is non-functional.
(Optional) Trace the residual `props.pointerEvents is deprecated` to its third-party source (probably SafeAreaView style flattening or LinearGradient) and silence/ignore if it's not from our code.
(Optional) Add a manual crash trigger or scripted obstacle to allow QA verification of the crash flash on web preview where sensors don't fire.
critical_code_review_comments: popupsRef.current is mutated but never read in the render tree -> dead state, indicates the new feature was only half-implemented.
game.tsx is ~1065 lines; consider splitting Overlay/TiltIndicator/BoostLines/ParallaxClouds into /app/frontend/src/ files for maintainability (>700-line guideline).
shadow*/textShadow* style keys: fully clean across game.tsx, index.tsx, settings.tsx, PaperPlane.tsx, Cloud.tsx (verified by grep).
updated_files: /app/frontend/app/game.tsx (3 pointerEvents prop -> style.pointerEvents migrations at lines 479, 494, 767)

success_rate.frontend: 85% (9 of 10 polish items verified; +50 score popup not rendered)

context_for_next_testing_agent: When E1 fixes the popupsRef rendering, re-test by playing /game, colliding with rings, and visually confirming a yellow +50 floats up for ~1s. Crash flash needs a real device or scripted obstacle since web sensors are flat. Console should still be free of shadow*/textShadow* deprecation warnings; 1 residual props.pointerEvents warning is from a third-party lib and is acceptable.
rca_of_issue: popupsRef.current is updated on ring collection (game.tsx ~line 316) and cleaned every frame (~line 268), but no JSX block consumes it during render. The previously defined styles.scorePopup is therefore unused. Mitigation: add a `popupsRef.current.map(p => <Text style={[styles.scorePopup, { left: planeScreenX - 30, top: planeScreenY - 40 - (now - p.t)/10, opacity: Math.max(0, 1 - (now - p.t)/1000) }]}>+{p.value}</Text>)` block inside the View tree, ideally inside the absoluteFill render layer above the plane.
